summarization.a: This XRD plot compares the structural characteristics of a film in its as-deposited state versus after annealing at 600 °C. The as-deposited sample shows a largely amorphous signature with minimal diffraction intensity, indicating poor crystallinity. After annealing, distinct diffraction peaks emerge, aligning closely with the SnO₂ reference pattern and confirming the formation of crystalline SnO₂. No peaks corresponding to SnO are observed, suggesting that annealing promotes full oxidation toward the Sn⁴⁺ phase rather than retaining Sn²⁺ in SnO. The figure highlights the critical role of thermal treatment in controlling crystallinity and phase composition.

data_extraction.a:
| 2θ(°) Peak Positions | As-Deposited | 600 °C Anneal | Reference |
|---------------------|--------------|----------------|-----------|
| ~26–27              | No peak      | Peak present   | SnO₂      |
| ~33                 | No peak      | Peak present   | SnO₂      |
| ~37                 | No peak      | Peak present   | SnO₂      |
| ~51                 | No peak      | Peak present   | SnO₂      |
| ~29, ~39            | No peak      | No peak        | SnO (reference only) |

a.question: What structural differences are evident between the as-deposited and 600 °C annealed samples in the XRD patterns?
a.answer: The as-deposited sample exhibits a nearly featureless pattern, indicating an amorphous or poorly crystalline structure. In contrast, the sample annealed at 600 °C shows several sharp diffraction peaks, which align well with the reference pattern for crystalline SnO₂. This suggests that annealing promotes crystallization and the formation of the SnO₂ phase, with no evidence of SnO remaining.

a.question: Does annealing at 600 °C increase the crystallinity of the film?
Which phase does the annealed sample resemble most closely based on the XRD pattern?
What are the benefits of annealing based on the XRD data presented?
a.answer: Improves crystallinity, promotes formation of SnO₂ phase, eliminates amorphous features, enhances phase purity.
